steps.title: Apply triangle angle sum property
steps.content:
The sum of all angles in a triangle equals $180^{\circ}$. With ∠F = $39^{\circ}$ and ∠G = $39^{\circ}$:
$$\angle F + \angle G + \angle H = 180^{\circ}$$
$$39^{\circ} + 39^{\circ} + \angle H = 180^{\circ}$$
$$78^{\circ} + \angle H = 180^{\circ}$$
$$\angle H = 102^{\circ}$$
B is correct.
